name: Laromestrocel aging frailty phase 2b trial
summary: A randomized, blinded, placebo-controlled phase 2b dose-finding trial testing a single infusion of allogeneic mesenchymal stem cells in older adults with aging frailty.
population: Ambulatory adults aged 70 to 85 with aging frailty and hypomobility

trial_details.horizon_note: Results are already in the public evidence layer, so this is not an open no-result watch item.
trial_details.why_it_matters: Results matter for Stem Cell Replacement and Transplantation because they can show whether the intervention changes function, biomarkers, and safety in Ambulatory adults aged 70 to 85 with aging frailty and hypomobility. That is still narrower than proof of broad aging reversal.
